rules: All time values must be stored and processed as absolute UTC timestamps.
Relative concepts such as 'end_of_day' must never be evaluated at runtime.
Access entitlement must be represented as fixed start_at and expires_at timestamps.
implementation_requirements: Use TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE or UTC ISO-8601 for all persisted time fields.
Compute access_expires_at at event handling time, not during access checks.
Access checks must only compare now_utc against persisted expires_at.

rules: Chargeback events always override refund and success outcomes.
Refund events override prior payment success but do not override chargebacks.
Event arrival order must never affect final access or subscription state.
implementation_requirements: Reconciliation must evaluate the full event history, not only the latest event.
Chargeback handling must immediately revoke access regardless of refund grace logic.
Refund grace logic must be skipped if a chargeback exists for the same billing cycle.

rules: Reconciliation must be idempotent per subscription_id.
Only one reconciliation result may be applied per projection_version.
Concurrent reconciliation attempts must not emit duplicate system events.
implementation_requirements: All reconciliation updates must use optimistic locking on projection_version.
Failed version updates must result in a no-op and safe retry.
Reconciliation processes must not rely on distributed locks.
sla_timer_rules: The inconsistent_state SLA timer may only start once per inconsistent window.
Subsequent events during inconsistent state must not reset the SLA timer.
Only audit logging is permitted while inconsistent state is active.

rules: Invariant simulation code must never be present in production binaries.
Simulation capabilities must be removed via build-time or dependency injection boundaries.
Runtime configuration flags alone are insufficient for simulation safety.
implementation_requirements: Invariant simulation modules must only be compiled in dev or test builds.
Production builds must not reference or import simulation code paths.
Middleware must detect and block any simulation trigger attempts in production.
security_response: Emit CRITICAL_SECURITY_ALERT on any detected simulation trigger in production.
Treat production simulation attempts as potential malicious interference.

rules: Mapping from external provider identifiers (e.g. stripe_customer_id) to internal user_id must occur only in identity_service.
No other service may infer, cache, or reconstruct identity mappings.
User account deletion must trigger external identity detachment.
implementation_requirements: Identity service must be the single source of truth for authentication and external provider linkage.
Stripe customer references must never be stored outside identity_service.
Authentication and authorization checks must not depend on subscription or payment state.
forbidden_patterns: Inferring user identity from payment or subscription records
Caching external identity mappings outside identity_service

rules: Notifications must be derived from events and must never mutate business state.
Each event may produce at most one notification per channel and recipient.
Notification delivery must be idempotent.
implementation_requirements: Notification intent creation must be deduplicated by source_event_id.
External delivery must be executed via an outbox mechanism.
Delivery retries must not create duplicate user-visible messages.
replay_safety_rules: Replay must never trigger notification delivery.
Outbox processing must be disabled during replay operations.

rules: Dispute services must never mutate subscription, access, or financial state.
Dispute outcomes must emit intent events rather than performing actions.
Human decisions must be traceable and auditable.
implementation_requirements: Evidence used in disputes must reference immutable snapshots.
Automated dispute resolution must be deterministic.
Escalation paths must be explicit and SLA-bound.
forbidden_patterns: Direct refund execution inside dispute service
Direct access modification inside dispute service

violation_handling: Block merge if forbidden patterns are detected.
Require explicit justification for any deviation.
Log and track guideline violations as technical debt.
